plan: Sell CRWD to exit the flagged holding. Cut SOXL and SPAL to zero to remove all leveraged ETFs. Trim SMH to reduce semiconductor concentration. Add to QQQI and SPY to increase income and broad index exposure. Watch PANW for a potential high risk/reward entry.
conservative.read: This conservative approach targets a modest ~1.5%/month, prioritizing capital preservation. Expect lower volatility and drawdown, but also limited upside potential due to reduced leverage and concentration.

plan: Sell CRWD to exit the flagged holding. Trim SOXL and SPAL to reduce leveraged ETF exposure. Reduce SMH to decrease semiconductor concentration. Add to QQQI for income. Buy PANW for a high risk/reward setup. Watch TECL for a potential tech leverage add.
balanced.read: This balanced approach aims for ~2.5%/month, focusing on risk-first growth. It balances broad exposure with quality tech and minor leverage. Expect moderate volatility and drawdown, with potential for steady growth.

plan: Sell CRWD to exit the flagged holding. Add to SOXL and SPAL to increase leveraged exposure. Increase SMH to lean into semiconductor concentration. Buy PANW and TECL for high risk/reward tech setups. Watch RTX for another high R/R opportunity.
aggressive.read: This aggressive approach targets ~4%/month, aiming for stretched growth. It involves significant leverage and concentration, leading to higher potential returns but also substantially increased drawdown risk. Leveraged ETFs decay daily, and this mode can lose capital very rapidly without strict stop discipline.

plan: Sell CRWD and PTNM to exit flagged and other holdings. Cut SOXL and SPAL to zero to remove all leveraged ETFs. Trim SMH to reduce high-beta exposure. Maximize QQQI for income. Add to SPY to maintain broad index exposure.
income.read: This income approach targets ~1%/month, prioritizing steady monthly distributions. It focuses on income and broad index ETFs, minimizing leverage and high-beta single names. While aiming for low drawdown, distributions are variable, and NAV can still decline in market selloffs.
